followup_notes.changes_from_prior_cycle: I substantially revise upward from weak_evidence because the arXiv v3 full text is available and contains methods, figures, diagnostics, and decomposition evidence that were absent from the supplied APS landing-page bundle. The main concerns now are not absence of the paper body, but scope of the ESPE-based interpretation, truncation control, and dependence on Supplemental Material.
invalid_points_from_other_reviewers: Gemini's fatal paper-level objection that the full text is inaccessible is no longer valid once the arXiv v3 full text is considered.
The claim that no supporting figures or data are available is invalid for the paper itself, though it was true of the supplied bundle.

Claude's abstract-level conceptual tension between pseudospin symmetry and magic-number generation is largely addressed in the full text, which tracks increasing spin-orbit splitting and decreasing pseudospin splitting as distinct diagnostics.
The popular-video periodic-table framing is not a technical objection to the paper, since it is not a claim made by the PRL article.
valid_points_from_other_reviewers: Claude was right that ESPEs and shell gaps are scheme-dependent and that the paper must distinguish scale-dependent effective spectra from scale-independent observables; the full text does acknowledge this caveat.
Claude was right that novelty must be judged against extensive pseudospin-symmetry and RMF literature.
Claude was right to question how independent the OBEP comparison is as evidence for robustness.
Gemini was right that the provided bundle itself was not review-ready because it contained only the APS abstract and authorization barrier.

main_claims.claim: Lowering the SRG resolution scale of chiral EFT interactions drives an evolution of nuclear effective single-particle shell structure.
main_claims.notes: The paper specifies an intrinsic NN+3N Hamiltonian, SRG flow, several chiral interaction families, HF calculations, and IMSRG/NO2B corrections.
main_claims.paper_location: Main text, Nuclear chiral interactions section and From spin to pseudospin symmetries section; arXiv PDF lines 59-133

main_claims.claim: In the representative 132Sn proton spectrum, spin-orbit splittings increase as lambda decreases and the pronounced shell gaps change from Z=34 to the traditional Z=28 and Z=50.
main_claims.notes: The text directly reports this trend and maps the spectra to Nilsson parameters. Full verification depends on inspecting the figure values and the Supplemental Material.
main_claims.paper_location: Main text and Fig. 1; arXiv PDF lines 124-158

main_claims.claim: Pseudospin symmetry emerges at low resolution, diagnosed by decreasing pseudospin splittings and Nilsson mu approaching 0.5.
main_claims.notes: This is now much better supported than in cycle 1 because the paper gives an explicit diagnostic. The breadth of the claim across nuclei/interactions still depends partly on supplemental figures.
main_claims.paper_location: Main text and Fig. 1; arXiv PDF lines 139-158

main_claims.claim: Three-nucleon forces, especially the two-pion c3 component in the EM-family analysis, are essential for enhancing the spin-orbit splitting at low resolution.
main_claims.notes: The paper provides a decomposition and quantitative percentages, but this is a specialized many-body calculation whose truncation and decomposition conventions require expert spot-checking.
main_claims.paper_location: Connection between nuclear forces and shell structure; Fig. 2; arXiv PDF lines 193-215 and 330-339

main_claims.claim: A similar spin-to-pseudospin evolution appears in relativistic one-boson-exchange potential calculations.
main_claims.notes: The OBEP/RHF comparison is real and useful, but it is not a fully SRG-consistent relativistic evolution; the paper itself says such an implementation is beyond scope.
main_claims.paper_location: Nuclear shell structure from relativistic studies; Fig. 3; arXiv PDF lines 340-371

main_claims.claim: The work offers a first-principles explanation for the origin of magic numbers and pseudospin symmetry.
main_claims.notes: The paper substantially supports a first-principles-to-effective-spectrum connection, but the wording is broad. It should not be read as a complete derivation of all empirical magic-number observables without the ESPE and scheme-dependence caveats.
main_claims.paper_location: Abstract and Conclusions; arXiv PDF lines 17-20 and 387-403

missing_assumptions.assumption: ESPE shell gaps and fitted Nilsson parameters are adequate proxies for the empirical magic-number phenomenon being explained.
missing_assumptions.paper_location: From spin to pseudospin symmetries section; arXiv PDF lines 112-158
missing_assumptions.why_it_matters: If the connection to separation energies, excitation energies, and other closed-shell observables is weak, the result is still interesting but the origin claim is overstated.

missing_assumptions.assumption: SRG-induced many-body effects and IMSRG truncation errors remain small over the resolution range used for the symmetry-transition plots.
missing_assumptions.why_it_matters: The reported scale evolution could be distorted by omitted induced operators or many-body truncation effects.

missing_assumptions.assumption: The Supplemental Material quantitatively supports the claimed generality across additional nuclei and interaction families.
missing_assumptions.paper_location: Multiple SM references in main text
missing_assumptions.why_it_matters: The main text highlights 132Sn heavily; the conclusion claims consistency across different nuclei and interactions.

missing_assumptions.assumption: The OBEP cutoff family is meaningfully comparable to the nonrelativistic SRG resolution trajectory.
missing_assumptions.paper_location: Relativistic studies section; arXiv PDF lines 340-371
missing_assumptions.why_it_matters: If the OBEP trend is largely due to fitting choices or known low-momentum universality, it is less independent evidence for the proposed mechanism.

missing_assumptions.assumption: Prior pseudospin-symmetry and low-momentum-interaction literature does not already contain the essential resolution-flow mechanism claimed as novel.
missing_assumptions.paper_location: Introduction and references; arXiv PDF lines 37-58 and 450-455
missing_assumptions.why_it_matters: The paper cites the classical pseudospin/RMF literature, but the novelty of the combined SRG-scale transition still needs expert literature judgment.

needs_human_spotcheck.question: Does the Supplemental Material substantiate the claimed generality across additional nuclei, interactions, and observables?
needs_human_spotcheck.why_model_cannot_resolve: The main text repeatedly refers to SM figures/equations for key evidence, and I only accessed the main arXiv paper.

needs_human_spotcheck.question: Are the SRG/IMSRG truncation choices, NO2B approximation, and treatment of induced higher-body terms adequate for the quantitative spin-orbit and pseudospin trends?
needs_human_spotcheck.why_model_cannot_resolve: This requires expert nuclear many-body assessment and probably inspection of implementation details beyond the PRL main text.

needs_human_spotcheck.question: Is the Nilsson-parameter mapping, especially the use of fixed hbar omega0 and fitted kappa, mu, robust enough to support the symmetry-transition interpretation?
needs_human_spotcheck.why_model_cannot_resolve: The main text gives the mapping but not enough detail for me to independently validate the fit choices and uncertainties.

needs_human_spotcheck.question: Does the paper's explanation connect to empirical magic-number signatures beyond effective spectra, such as separation-energy discontinuities and excitation energies?
needs_human_spotcheck.why_model_cannot_resolve: The main text emphasizes ESPEs and says genuine observables are invariant under resolution changes; a domain expert should judge whether the observable-level connection is sufficient.

needs_human_spotcheck.question: How novel is the proposed resolution-scale mechanism relative to prior SRG/V_low-k, 3N spin-orbit, and pseudospin-symmetry work?
needs_human_spotcheck.why_model_cannot_resolve: The paper cites relevant literature, but novelty assessment requires a deeper field survey than I can certify here.

novelty_assessment: The novelty is stronger than I judged in cycle 1. The paper is not claiming pseudospin symmetry itself is new; it cites Arima, Hecht-Adler, Ginocchio, and later RMF reviews. The plausibly novel contribution is the resolution-scale narrative connecting high-resolution chiral forces to empirical low-resolution shell-model structure, with a concrete spin-to-pseudospin crossover and a 3N-force decomposition. That seems promising and significant, but the precise novelty relative to V_low-k, previous SRG shell-structure work, and earlier 3N spin-orbit analyses needs human domain checking.
score_rationale: The score rises from cycle 1 because the full arXiv paper provides concrete calculations and diagnostics, and the result looks genuinely promising for nuclear-structure theory. I do not score it in the 8-10 range because the strongest claims rely on ESPE interpretation, supplemental evidence, and specialized many-body truncation control that I cannot independently certify.
summary: I revise my prior abstract-only judgment upward after finding the arXiv v3 full text at https://arxiv.org/abs/2504.09148. The paper is not merely a paywalled abstract: it gives a concrete HF/SRG and IMSRG analysis of effective single-particle energies, Nilsson-parameter diagnostics, a 3N-force decomposition, and an OBEP/RHF comparison. I still do not certify the full calculation. The strongest remaining caveat is that the central origin claim is primarily an effective-spectrum explanation, with several key robustness and error-control details pushed to Supplemental Material.

technical_objections.evidence: The paper explicitly notes that ESPEs are not unambiguous single-nucleon observables and that genuine observables remain invariant under resolution changes. This is an honest caveat, but it narrows what the title-level origin claim establishes.
technical_objections.objection: The magic-number explanation is framed mainly through effective single-particle energies, which are scheme-dependent interpretive quantities rather than strict observables.
technical_objections.paper_location: From spin to pseudospin symmetries section; arXiv PDF lines 112-123 and 157-159

technical_objections.evidence: The definition of the diagonalized single-particle Hamiltonian, additional interaction families, ground-state invariance checks, 90Zr trends, Ca-isotope trends, and some onset-of-magic-number evidence are all referenced as being in the SM.
technical_objections.objection: Several central robustness claims are delegated to Supplemental Material not included in the review bundle.
technical_objections.paper_location: References to SM around arXiv PDF lines 132-134, 158-164, 335-337, 358-359, and 379-381

technical_objections.evidence: The paper says two interactions evolve NN and 3N consistently, the EM family evolves NN with refitted 3N LECs, and IMSRG uses NO2B with noniterative higher-order checks reported as small via private communication. That may be acceptable, but it is not something I can certify from the main text.
technical_objections.objection: The ab initio strength of the claim depends on truncation control for SRG/IMSRG evolution, induced many-body terms, and NO2B approximations.
technical_objections.paper_location: Method section; arXiv PDF lines 103-111 and 127-130; reference note at line 511

technical_objections.evidence: The EM-family trend is described as weaker, N2LOGO(394) is already low resolution and changes little, and the relativistic OBEP comparison uses cutoff-varied fitted potentials rather than a fully consistent relativistic SRG/IMSRG evolution.
technical_objections.objection: The universality language is stronger than the evidence visible in the main text.
technical_objections.paper_location: Robustness discussion and Conclusions; arXiv PDF lines 160-168, 340-371, and 397-400

technical_objections.evidence: The paper tracks monotonic changes in spin-orbit and pseudospin splittings and Nilsson parameters. I did not see a formal threshold or phase-transition-like criterion in the main text.
technical_objections.objection: The word transition appears to denote a continuous crossover in fitted symmetry diagnostics, not a sharply defined transition.
technical_objections.paper_location: Fig. 1 and associated text; arXiv PDF lines 151-158 and 330-339
